title: Delicate Malty Norwegian Barley Pilaf
description: A malty, fluffy, and comforting Norwegian dish featuring starch and shio-cure, perfect for a cold winter night. This keto-friendly recipe is cooked to perfection in a bamboo steamer.
instructions: Rinse the pearl barley in a fine mesh strainer and drain well.
In a medium saucepan, bring the water to a boil over high heat.
Add the malted milk powder and shio-cure salt to the boiling water and stir to combine.
Add the rinsed barley to the saucepan and reduce the heat to low.
Cover the saucepan with a tight-fitting lid and simmer for 20 minutes.
While the barley is cooking, melt the unsalted butter in a small saucepan over low heat.
Once the barley is cooked, fluff it with a fork and stir in the melted butter and chopped parsley.
If desired, add the granulated sweetener and stir to combine.
Transfer the barley mixture to a bamboo steamer lined with parchment paper.
Steam the barley over boiling water for 10 minutes, or until heated through.
